title: The Whispering Walls
storySnippet: Ancient walls whisper forgotten words to Lira, each one a key to unlocking the courtyard's deeper secrets.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Finding the lost puppy on the same day it went missing was a ___ coincidence.
data.definition: Fortuitous: happening by chance, especially in a lucky or unexpected way.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ captain demanded that every crew member follow orders without question.
data.definition: Imperious: assuming power or authority in a domineering way; arrogant and commanding.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ spread of the invasive plant species threatened the entire native ecosystem.
data.definition: Insidious: proceeding in a gradual, subtle way but with harmful effects.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: My ___ uncle can talk for hours about any subject, from history to cooking.
data.definition: Loquacious: tending to talk a great deal; very talkative.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: In a ___ gesture, the winner shared her prize money with the runner-up.
data.definition: Magnanimous: very generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or less powerful person.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ servant always agreed with the king, bowing and flattering at every turn.
data.definition: Obsequious: excessively eager to please or obey; overly submissive and flattering.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Lack of sleep can have ___ effects on a student's ability to concentrate in class.
data.definition: Pernicious: having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ toddler refused to put on his shoes no matter how much his parents asked.
data.definition: Recalcitrant: stubbornly resistant to authority or control; uncooperative.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ elder of the village was known for giving wise advice to anyone who asked.
data.definition: Sagacious: having or showing keen mental discernment and good judgment; wise.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: Smartphones have become so ___ that it is rare to meet someone who does not own one.
data.definition: Ubiquitous: present, appearing, or found everywhere; extremely widespread.
